title: Bounded LLM Adjudication Advisory Trial
decision_question: Can bounded LLM adjudication improve top-action help rate and repair guidance quality enough to justify latency and cost after static and semantic narrowing?
hypothesis: A bounded adjudication pass over structured evidence bundles can rerank ambiguous candidates, suppress low-value findings, and improve repair packets without replacing static and semantic narrowing.

variants.name: Bounded advisory rerank
variants.description: Use the LLM only to rerank ambiguous candidates and suppress likely low-value leads from structured evidence bundles.
variants.policy_delta: Structured evidence bundle, advisory only, full audit logging.

variants.name: Bounded repair guidance
variants.description: Keep ranking static, but use the LLM to strengthen likely fix sites, repair guidance, and verification notes.
variants.policy_delta: Structured evidence bundle, guidance only, no raw repo scan.

exit_bar: Keep the LLM outside the initial narrowing path and feed it structured evidence bundles only.
Log every input, output, and decision path for audit.
Do not enable by default unless help rate improves enough to justify added latency and cost.
